title: Add a --since flag to the chlog CLI

facts.text: chlog is a Node.js CLI tool already published to npm.
The new flag must be named --since and accept a date like 2026-01-01.
Default behavior (no flags) must remain exactly last-tag-to-HEAD.
Output must remain valid Markdown matching the existing CHANGELOG.md format.
A short -s alias for --since would be nice to have.
The date parser should also accept full ISO 8601 timestamps, not just plain dates.
An invalid date should produce an error listing the accepted formats.
The feature must run identically on Windows and Linux CI runners.
A one-line summary of the matched commit count should be printed.
Commits from bot accounts (username ending in [bot]) must be excluded.

ambiguities: Whether --since implies a paired --until flag or only an open-ended range to HEAD.
Whether relative date values like '7d' are in scope for this release or only absolute dates.
What happens when a future date is passed: error out or silently return zero commits.
Whether merge commits should be included or excluded from the --since output.
latent: The tool must make no network calls at all -- the CI runners that execute chlog are air-gapped.
The flag must ship in Friday's release, which is at most a minor version bump, so nothing may break for existing callers.
